postData.contentHtml:
The southern Philippines has been rocked by a powerful 7.8 magnitude earthquake, triggering widespread devastation and a mounting humanitarian crisis. The latest reports indicate that the death toll has tragically risen to between 45 and 46 people, with over 630 individuals sustaining injuries and 17 still reported missing.

Recovery operations are severely complicated by the relentless barrage of aftershocks, which continue to jolt the region. These seismic tremors pose a significant risk to rescue workers and further destabilize damaged infrastructure, making efforts to search for survivors and provide aid increasingly challenging.

Beyond the immediate casualties, the earthquake has forced more than 32,000 people from their homes, creating a dire situation for displaced families now facing uncertainty and hardship. Emergency services are working tirelessly to provide shelter, food, and medical assistance, but the scale of the disaster is immense.

Officials on the ground have expressed grave concerns that the death toll may continue to rise as rescue teams reach more remote areas and as the full extent of the damage becomes clear. The international community is closely monitoring the situation, with calls for support to assist the Philippines in its arduous recovery journey.
postData.title: Philippines Grapples with Aftershocks as Deadly Quake's Toll Rises
postData.summary: A powerful 7.8 magnitude earthquake has devastated the southern Philippines, leaving at least 45 dead and hindering recovery efforts with persistent aftershocks. Thousands are displaced as officials warn the casualty count could still climb.

sources.title: Aftershocks hamper recovery work after Philippines earthquake kills 45
A magnitude 7.8 quake killed at least 45 in the southern Philippines and triggered a tsunami
Death toll from Philippines quake rises to 46
Aftershocks complicate recovery efforts after deadly quake in southern Philippines
Hundreds of aftershocks jolt Philippines as officials say death toll could rise
Philippines earthquake displaces 32,000 as tsunami fears spread
